title: FLL Autonomous Mission Challenge
subtitle: Master autonomous programming for First LEGO League
description: Design and program an autonomous robot that can complete multiple FLL mission objectives. This project integrates motor control, sensor reading, navigation, and mission sequencing to create a reliable autonomous routine.
learningObjectives: Implement precise motor control and sensor integration

overview.context: In FLL competitions, autonomous missions are worth points and demonstrate your robot's ability to operate independently. A well-programmed autonomous routine can complete multiple objectives in 30 seconds. This project challenges you to create an efficient, reliable mission program.
challenges: Precise navigation in a defined field

phases.title: Planning and Design
phases.description: Analyze the mission objectives and design your approach

tasks.title: Study Mission Objectives
tasks.description: Review the FLL game manual and identify all mission objectives, their locations, point values, and success criteria. Create a prioritized list based on difficulty and point value.
resources: Official game field dimensions and layout
Video walkthroughs of successful autonomous routines
tasks.deliverable: Mission priority list and field diagram with annotations

tasks.title: Develop Robot Strategy
tasks.description: Design a robot movement strategy that efficiently completes high-value missions. Consider field layout, movement efficiency, and mission sequencing. Sketch your planned paths.
tasks.deliverable: Detailed strategy document with path diagrams and timing estimates

tasks.title: Calibrate Your Robot
tasks.description: Test and calibrate all sensors and motors. Establish baseline values for distance, rotation, color thresholds, and motor speeds that you'll use in programming.
tasks.deliverable: Calibration data document with all sensor thresholds and motor profiles

phases.title: Foundation Programming
phases.description: Build reusable movement functions and basic navigation

tasks.title: Implement Drive Functions
tasks.description: Create functions for basic movement: move forward a specific distance, turn a specific angle, and move with precision using encoder feedback.
tasks.codeTemplate:
from pybricks.hubs import EV3Brick
from pybricks.ev3devices import Motor, ColorSensor, UltrasonicSensor
from pybricks.parameters import Port, Stop, Direction
from pybricks.robotics import DriveBase

# Initialize
ev3 = EV3Brick()
left_motor = Motor(Port.B, positive_direction=Direction.COUNTERCLOCKWISE)
right_motor = Motor(Port.C)
gyro = GyroSensor(Port.2)

# Define your drive functions
def move_forward(distance_cm):
    # Your implementation
    pass

def turn(angle_degrees):
    # Your implementation
    pass

def move_to_position(x, y):
    # Your implementation
    pass
tasks.deliverable: Working movement functions tested on the actual field

tasks.title: Develop Line Following
tasks.description: Implement accurate line following using the color sensor. Calibrate color thresholds and implement a PID-like controller for smooth tracking.
tasks.codeTemplate:
def follow_line(target_color=50, distance_mm=500, speed=300):
    """Follow a line for a specified distance"""
    # Implement proportional control
    # error = target_color - color_sensor.reflection()
    # adjust motor speeds based on error
    pass
tasks.deliverable: Line following function that reliably tracks lines on the field

tasks.title: Create Navigation Helpers
tasks.description: Build utility functions for common navigation tasks: move to a colored region, stop at a distance, detect obstacles, etc.
tasks.deliverable: Set of reusable navigation utility functions

phases.title: Mission Implementation
phases.description: Program individual missions and test thoroughly

tasks.title: Program Mission Sequence
tasks.description: Implement code for your prioritized mission list. Test each mission individually, then in sequence. Handle failures gracefully with error recovery.
tasks.deliverable: Functional autonomous program that completes multiple missions

tasks.title: Test and Refine
tasks.description: Run your autonomous routine 10+ times on the actual competition field. Record success/failure and time for each run. Refine based on results.
tasks.deliverable: Test results log showing reliability and performance metrics

tasks.title: Implement Error Recovery
tasks.description: Add error detection and recovery strategies. For example: detect if a mission failed and attempt alternative approach or skip to next mission.
tasks.deliverable: Autonomous program with robust error handling

tasks.title: Optimize for Points
tasks.description: Analyze mission point values and adjust sequence to maximize total points. Consider time constraints and mission interdependencies.
tasks.deliverable: Optimized mission sequence with time allocation

tasks.title: Test Reliability
tasks.description: Run your complete autonomous routine 20+ times, tracking success rate. Achieve 90%+ reliability before competition.

tasks.title: Final Calibration
tasks.description: Make final tweaks to motor speeds, sensor thresholds, and timings. Ensure your robot performs consistently across different environmental conditions.
tasks.deliverable: Final calibration settings and performance report

phases.title: Documentation and Presentation
phases.description: Document your work for competition judging

tasks.title: Create Engineering Notebook
tasks.description: Document your design process, decisions, challenges, and solutions. Include diagrams, code snippets, and photos of your robot.
tasks.deliverable: Complete engineering notebook with all phases documented

tasks.title: Code Documentation
tasks.description: Add comprehensive comments to your code. Explain the purpose of each function, parameters, and key algorithm steps.

tasks.title: Prepare Presentation
tasks.description: Create a presentation explaining your autonomous strategy, technical implementation, and results. Be prepared to discuss decisions and answer questions.

tutorials.title: Pybricks Motor Control
tutorials.description: Official guide to motor control in Pybricks

tutorials.title: Sensor Integration
tutorials.description: How to read and use different sensor types

templates.title: Starter Movement Functions
templates.description: Basic forward, turn, and drive functions to build upon

templates.title: Mission Program Structure
templates.description: Template for organizing mission code with error handling

successCriteria.description: Robot operates independently for 30 seconds without human intervention
Successfully completes at least 3 different mission objectives
Clean, well-organized, commented code with reusable functions
Achieves 80%+ success rate across multiple test runs
Complete engineering notebook and code documentation
successCriteria.evidence: Engineering notebook and inline code comments

autonomousFunctionality.excellent: Robot completes 5+ missions reliably; operates smoothly with minimal failures
autonomousFunctionality.good: Robot completes 3-4 missions; operates reliably with occasional failures
autonomousFunctionality.satisfactory: Robot completes 2-3 missions; operation is inconsistent but functional
autonomousFunctionality.needsImprovement: Robot completes 1 or fewer missions; significant reliability issues

codeQuality.excellent: Well-organized, modular, well-commented; uses functions and error handling effectively
codeQuality.good: Organized with good function use; most sections well-commented
codeQuality.satisfactory: Functional code; some organization and documentation
codeQuality.needsImprovement: Disorganized, difficult to follow; minimal documentation

engineering.excellent: Clear strategy, proper calibration, thorough testing; design decisions well-justified
engineering.good: Good strategy with calibration and testing; most decisions explained
engineering.satisfactory: Basic strategy and testing; limited explanation of decisions
engineering.needsImprovement: Minimal planning or testing; decisions not documented

documentation.excellent: Comprehensive notebook with detailed explanations and technical diagrams

notes: This is a substantial project that mirrors real FLL competition demands. Work incrementally, test thoroughly, and don't hesitate to iterate. The engineering notebook is as important as the code - document your thinking and decision-making process.
